ai_instructions.role: Toyota Production System production-planning standards author
ai_instructions.task: Produce a feasible production schedule in Excel format
framework: - Product: name the SKU, full technical spec, and the customer or order reference it ties back to
- Target Volume: state the quantity to produce, the demand basis behind it, and the safety-stock buffer to hold
- Cycle: set the planned run length, the start and end dates, and the shift pattern that covers it
- Materials: pull the bill of materials and confirm full kitting before releasing the line
- Dates: lay out the exact daily timing, the operation sequence, and every handoff point between stations
- Target: set the planned output volume and tie it directly to customer delivery commitments
- Schedule: lay out the production sequence, week-level milestones, and daily work targets per line
- Resources: assign machines, fix labor headcount, and balance the line across stations
- Capacity Constraints: cap machine and labor capacity, and flag the bottleneck step that gates throughput
- Material Kitting: schedule arrival times and lock the full-kit cutoff before line start
- Balancing: identify the bottleneck and lift equipment utilization across the line
- Exceptions: define how to respond to equipment faults, material shortages, and rush-order insertion
- Metrics: track schedule attainment, machine utilization, and on-time delivery rate every shift

ai_instructions.task: Produce a data-rich production attainment report
framework: - Target: state the planned production volume and the assumptions you used to set it
- Actual: report finished units produced and the attainment percentage versus plan for the period
- Attainment %: calculate the ratio of actual to planned and the variance amount in units
- Variance: trace the root cause of the gap and name the primary drivers across material, machine, and labor
- Actions: lay out the corrective direction and the recovery plan, with named owners and dates

ai_instructions.task: Produce a targeted capacity expansion plan
framework: - Bottleneck: identify the constraint step and quantify the current shortfall in capacity versus demand
- Actions: propose technology upgrade options and line-flow redesigns that lift the constraint
- Investment: itemize equipment costs, additional labor, and total capital required to execute
- Expected Lift: project the capacity gain in units and the efficiency improvement percentage
- Timeline: phase the rollout and set acceptance milestones for each phase
- Validation: measure the actual lift, compare to forecast, and confirm attainment of the target
- Standardize: lock the new flow, update work instructions, and roll out training to every shift

ai_instructions.task: Produce a robust production dispatch plan
framework: - Order Priority: rank orders by urgency tier, customer deadline, and penalty exposure if missed
- Machine Assignment: define dispatch rules and balance the load across the line in real time
- Staffing: assign shifts and match operator skill to the operation each station requires
- Exceptions: spell out how to insert rush orders and how to respond to a machine fault
- Changeover: minimize setup time per part and sequence runs to cut total changeover time
- Rules: weight priority by urgency, customer value, and due-date risk for each order
- Dynamic Adjust: define insertion logic, reschedule triggers, and downstream sync at every step
- Steps: map the process flow and call out key motions, grip points, and station transitions
- Parameters: specify temperature, pressure, speed, and dimensional tolerance for every operation
- Quality: name inspection points and pass/fail criteria with measurable thresholds at each gate
- Safety: list required PPE and the emergency-stop and evacuation procedure for the area
- Tools: identify jigs, fixtures, and gauges by part number and storage location
- Steps: list the full sequence of actions and assign a role to own each step
- Owners: name the execution role, support roles, and the escalation contact when things slip
- Control Points: define quality gates with metrics and pass/fail thresholds at each one
- Exceptions: explain how to spot deviations and the handling protocol that follows
- Forms: spell out filling rules, retention period, and the archival location for every form
- BOM: list every line item and the standard usage per finished unit produced
- Inventory: capture on-hand quantities plus in-transit and committed stock per SKU
- POs: set reorder quantity, order placement date, and the expected receipt window
- Lead Time: combine vendor production lead time with transit days for a realistic delivery date
- Safety Stock: define the floor level and the reorder alert threshold for each SKU
- Order Sort: rank orders by priority rule, delivery deadline, and value tier
- Load: target machine utilization and fill idle slots with low-priority work
- Changeover: minimize setup time and batch similar parts to cut total changeover hours
- On-Time: set the OTIF target and identify slip risk for each order in flight
- Relief: offload work to alternate lines and schedule planned overtime when needed
- ID: name the constraint step and quantify the capacity gap versus current demand
- Load %: report machine hours used and labor saturation rate at each station
- Kaizen: propose capacity-add options and line redesign moves to break the constraint
- Expected Lift: project throughput improvement and efficiency gain from each option
- Evidence: cite historical data and simulation model results that support the recommendation
- Priority: rank actions by ROI and implementation difficulty before sequencing them
- Validation: measure before and after, then confirm the constraint is gone
- Items: list inspection points and the dimensional and functional specs each must meet
- Sampling: set the sample size and the statistical method, whether AQL or 100% inspection
- Pass/Fail: define upper and lower limits and the acceptance criteria for each measurement
- Records: specify the data entry format and the reporting cadence and recipients
- Frequency: set the sampling cadence per lot and the lot-size triggers that adjust it

ai_instructions.type: Routine Report → upward visibility, show value
framework: - First-Pass Yield: report yield per lot and the rolling FPY trend over the reporting window
- Defect Distribution: break down defects by type and location on the product or process
- Root Cause: analyze the underlying cause and assign accountability to a named owner
- Actions: list corrective actions taken and preventive actions planned, with target dates
- Trends: compare month-over-month and year-over-year to spot drift early
- Top Issues: list major defect categories with frequency counts and impact
- Tracking: report closure status and verify each action actually solved the problem
- Current State: name the specific waste or productivity loss with hard measurements (cycle time, defect rate, takt-time variance)
- Target: set the improvement metric, the measurable outcome you're aiming for, and the deadline to hit it
- Actions: list the kaizen steps in execution order, with the owner and resource ask for each
- Validation: measure before and after, then calculate ROI to confirm the gain is real
- Standardize: update work instructions and lock in the change so it cannot drift back
- Spread: identify other lines where this fix applies and lay out the replication plan
- Standardize: revise the work instruction, update the flow, and train every operator on the change
- Team: form a cross-functional team and define each member's role on the investigation
- Problem: describe the issue clearly and bound its scope and impact with data
- Containment: take interim action to protect the customer while you investigate the root cause
- Root Cause: dig down to the true cause and verify it with reproducible evidence
- Permanent: design the corrective solution and lay out the implementation plan
- Prevent: make systemic changes and deploy the fix horizontally to similar processes
- Team: name the accountable owner and the cross-functional members supporting the fix
- Cause: identify the direct cause and verify the root cause with data, not opinion
- Corrective: apply the specific fix and update the standard so the change sticks
- Preventive: design an error-proofing mechanism or poka-yoke to block recurrence
- Validation: run a re-test plan and set a long-run monitoring window to confirm closure
- Items: name the metric measured and the sampling location on the process or product
- Results: report measured values with charts that show the distribution shape
- vs Spec: compare to upper and lower limits and issue the pass or fail verdict per item
- Verdict: state the overall pass or fail and the disposition of any nonconforming material
- Method: list instruments used, calibration status, and the test procedure followed
- Stats: report aggregate counts and describe the distribution shape and central tendency
- Trends: identify the pattern over time and the suspected causal drivers behind it
- Anomalies: flag statistical outliers and early warning signals that need attention
- Priorities: rank top items by Pareto and recommend where to put resources first
- Incident: describe the phenomenon and report frequency and severity with hard data
- Cause Hunt: brainstorm possible causes, then verify or rule out each one with evidence
- Root Cause: confirm the root cause and document the supporting evidence chain
- CAPA: lay out the corrective action plan and the preventive action plan, with owners
- Closure: verify effectiveness and standardize the fix so it sticks across shifts
- Current Flow: map every step of the current process and measure cycle time per step
- Bottlenecks: identify wait points and call out the waste types you find at each one
- Plan: apply ECRS — eliminate, combine, rearrange, simplify — to each step in the flow
- Results: quantify the efficiency improvement and the cost savings achieved
- Standardize: document the new flow and roll out training to every operator on the line

ai_instructions.task: Produce a quality strike team plan
framework: - Targets: set the target metric, the expected lift, and the deadline to hit it
- Team: build a cross-functional team and define each member's role and decision authority
- Plan: lay out phases with milestones and decision gates between them
- Lock-in: fix the process and update standards documentation so the change survives
- Rewards: design incentives and a recognition program for team members who deliver
- Regulations: list applicable workplace-safety standards and the industry-specific rules that apply
- Hazard ID: identify hazard sources and run a risk assessment for each one
- Response: walk through first-aid steps, evacuation procedure, and escalation contacts
- Case Studies: review real incidents and pull out lessons learned that workers can apply
- Assessment: combine a written exam with a practical demonstration test to confirm competence
- Standards: select the relevant ISO standards (9001, 14001, or industry-specific) for certification
- Docs: build the quality manual, procedures, and work-record templates the auditor will sample
- Internal Audit: schedule the audit plan and track every nonconformity through to closure
- Certification: pick the certification body and prepare the team for the on-site audit
- CI: set the management review cycle and lay out the system evolution roadmap
- Receiving: run a quality check on arrival and verify count against the shipping document
- Paperwork: issue the goods received note and enter the receipt into the ERP system
- Putaway: apply slotting rules and set the putaway path for each pallet
- System: sync inventory in real time and confirm every move with a barcode scan
- Exceptions: define refusal criteria and the escalation path for damaged or short shipments
- Trigger: define the order release event and the approval prerequisite that unlocks picking
- Picking: enforce FIFO and use an optimized picking path to cut walk time
- Check: verify outbound quantity and run a quality spot-check before sealing the carton
- Docs: generate the packing slip and the proof-of-delivery template for the carrier
- FIFO: track batches and enforce expiry dates so old stock ships first
- Slotting: zone the warehouse by velocity and lay out a flow-through layout from dock to dock
- Flows: design the inbound dock plan and the picking route to minimize travel
- Accuracy: set a count variance target and reconcile system to physical on every cycle count
- Efficiency: identify automation opportunities and the system upgrades that pay back fastest
- Cycle Count: build the periodic count plan and set the rolling count cadence per zone
- ROI: total the investment cost, project expected return, and calculate payback period
- Plan: phase implementation with milestones and acceptance gates between phases
- Classification: run an ABC analysis and report turn rate per SKU
- Turn: measure inbound and outbound frequency and days on hand for each item
- Move: place fast-movers near the pick face and push slow-movers to the back
- Labels: re-tag locations and update the system map so pickers find every SKU
- Map: redraw the slot grid layout and sync it with the WMS overnight
- Route: plan the path and report total mileage and number of stops
- Fleet: match vehicle type to load and lay out the load plan for each truck
- Cost: estimate fuel, tolls, driver labor, and accessorials for the lane
- On-Time: set ETA at each stop and the exception escalation plan when one slips
- Insurance: confirm cargo coverage and walk through the claims process
- Contingency: build a weather plan, breakdown response, and accident protocol
- KPIs: track on-time delivery, cargo intactness, cost per mile, and customer satisfaction
- KPIs: measure dispatch on time, on-time delivery, and miles per gallon for every driver
- Sources: pull data from GPS telematics, fuel receipts, and maintenance records
- Scoring: weight each KPI, define scoring tiers, and document the math openly
- Incentives: pay a safety bonus and a fuel-efficiency award to top performers
- Cadence: score monthly and review with each driver every quarter
- Zones: lay out the delivery grid and map demand heat by hour of day
- SLA: set the delivery time window and the penalty for missing it
- Exceptions: define the playbook for lost or damaged packages and customer complaints
- Customer Comms: place a courtesy call and send SMS notifications at every milestone
- Proof of Delivery: capture an electronic signature and a photo at the drop point

ai_instructions.task: Produce a reverse logistics returns workflow
framework: - Request: let the customer initiate the return and categorize the reason in the form
- Inspection: run a quality check on the returned item and assign liability to the right party
- Putaway: route the item back to inventory or to the scrap path based on condition
- Refund: route through the approval flow and refund to the original payment method
- Liability: determine whether the vendor or carrier owns the loss before charging back
- Turn: report inventory turn rate and days on hand for every category
- Slow-Moving: pull the aged stock report and assign a root cause to each slow SKU
- Safety Stock: set the reorder point and min/max levels per SKU based on demand variability
- Recommendations: propose inter-store transfers and a markdown plan for stuck inventory
- Classification: tier SKUs by ABC and overlay a profitability tier on top
- BOM: list every line item with its full technical specification
- Quantity: set the batch size and the total annual purchase volume per item
- Timing: schedule the PO release date and the required delivery date
- Budget: set the unit price target and the total spend ceiling for the year
- Vendor: report the bid result and the rationale for the selection you made
- Structure: break out unit price, logistics cost, and taxes and duties for each item
- Trend: review price history and forecast the forward curve for the category
- Savings: identify negotiation levers and alternative materials that cut cost
- Strategy: name the pressure points to push and the batching opportunities to consolidate
- Quote Log: log multi-source quotes and rank them on a scoring grid
- Dimensions: score quality, on-time delivery, price, and service responsiveness for each vendor
- Scoring: define each metric, set the scale, and explain the adjustment logic
- Sources: pull data from incoming QC records and delivery statistics
- Tiers: classify vendors A/B/C/D with clear entry and exit thresholds
- Cadence: run a full annual review and update the score every quarter
- Credentials: collect business licenses and verify any industry certifications they claim
- Capacity: list equipment and confirm the maximum monthly output they can sustain
- QMS: review process documentation and confirm in-line and final testing capability
- Financials: check revenue trend and the balance sheet health for stability
- References: contact past customers and review comparable projects they delivered

ai_instructions.task: Produce a vendor delivery analysis report
ai_instructions.type: Routine Report → upward visibility, show value
framework: - OTD: report on-time delivery rate and average delay days per vendor
- Completeness: track order fill rate and the frequency of shortages
- Quality: report incoming pass rate and return-merchandise rate per shipment
- Improvements: lay out the vendor coaching plan and start backup vendor development
- Window: take a monthly snapshot and run a quarterly trend review
- Cadence: update scores monthly and run a full annual review every December
- KPIs: score quality, delivery, cost competitiveness, and service for every active vendor
- Scoring: weight each KPI, calculate the total, and band the result into tiers
- Use: shift share to better vendors and replace the ones that consistently fail
- Follow-up: issue corrective action requests and re-verify the fix on the next cycle
- Parties: name the buyer and supplier entities with full legal names and addresses
- Product: list product names and reference the technical specifications by document number
- Pricing: state unit price, total amount, and payment terms in clear language
- Delivery/Acceptance: cite the Incoterms and define acceptance standards and the inspection process
- Default: list triggering events and set the liquidated damages amount per breach
- Target: set the target price per unit and the target commercial terms you want to land
- BATNA: define the walk-away price ceiling and the latest acceptable delivery date
- Leverage: name our negotiation strengths and the counterparty weak spots to press on
- Concessions: prepare the trade-offs you can offer and the tiered fallbacks behind them
- Alternatives: line up a second-source vendor and a substitute material before you sit down
- Nodes: map every step from requisition through approval to order placement
- Approval Efficiency: count approval layers and measure average cycle time end to end
- System Support: confirm the e-procurement platform is live and integrated with ERP
- Controls: build in compliance checks and segregation of duties at every approval gate
- SoD: set approval limits per role and separate buyer and approver responsibilities
- E2E: map the end-to-end flow from raw-material supplier to end customer
- Bottleneck: identify breakpoints and measure queue and wait times at each one
- Inventory: report raw material, work-in-process, and finished goods levels at every stage
- Cost: total procurement spend, logistics cost, and warehousing cost across the chain
- Risk: assess supply disruption likelihood and demand variability for the planning horizon
- Info Sharing: share order data, demand forecast, and inventory visibility with key vendors
- Planning: build a collaborative production plan and align the replenishment schedule
- Inventory: deploy vendor-managed inventory and just-in-time delivery for high-volume parts
- Joint Kaizen: launch shared cost-reduction projects and joint quality-improvement work
- Integration: set up EDI exchange and API connectivity for real-time data flow

ai_instructions.task: Produce a smart sortation system ops guide
framework: - Login: define user credentials and assign role-based permissions for every operator
- Rules: set the wave release strategy and the sortation routing logic per chute
- Exceptions: define missed-scan recovery and the manual override procedure
- Maintenance: build the cleaning schedule and the repair escalation path
- Permissions: assign roles and set audit log retention per compliance requirements

ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Dish Name: craft a creative name that carries a cultural or regional reference
- Ingredients: name the hero ingredient and call out provenance and farm name where notable
- Taste: describe the flavor profile and the texture in language a guest can taste
- Price: list per-item price and disclose portion or unit size clearly
- Allergens: list every allergen and add a warning where the dish carries real risk
- Plating: write the plating style guide and call out garnish placement on every dish
- Angle: specify top-down, side, and macro detail shots for each menu item
- Post: define color grading direction and set retouching limits to keep food honest
- Layout: set the image-to-text ratio and the whitespace usage on every page
- Color: harmonize food color with the background palette across the menu
- Food Cost: report cost of goods sold percentage and waste percentage per category
- Labor: total back-of-house and front-of-house wages plus the benefits load
- Operating: itemize rent, utilities, depreciation, and amortization for the period
- Breakeven: calculate the breakeven revenue point and the margin of safety against it
- Personal Hygiene: require a current health card, enforce handwashing protocol, and standardize the uniform
- Environment: set the kitchen sanitation schedule and the trash handling procedure
- Storage: define temperature zones and the steps to prevent cross-contamination
- Sanitize Log: record sanitation time and chemical concentration on every cycle
- Frequency: set the daily, weekly, and spot-check inspection cadence in writing
- Scheduling: assign shifts and track tardiness with clear consequences in the handbook
- Service Standards: write the greeting script and set grooming requirements for every shift
- Training: build the onboarding curriculum and the recurring re-certification schedule
- Rewards: launch a recognition program and a points-based incentive system
- Grooming: set the dress code and personal appearance standards in plain language
- Goals: set the revenue target, table turnover rate, and customer satisfaction score for the year
- Flow: streamline the ordering process and speed up checkout at peak hours
- Cost Control: cut food waste and tighten the labor cost ratio without dropping service
- Marketing: build the campaign calendar and the channel mix for the season
- KPIs: list dimensions tracked and set weekly and monthly targets per dimension
- Greeting: write the welcome script and the seating procedure every host follows
- Ordering: build the recommendation script and the order confirmation step at the table
- Delivery: announce each dish at the table and standardize plate placement
- Farewell: write the thank-you script and run a satisfaction check before the guest leaves
- Complaints: train the listen, apologize, fix sequence with clear manager escalation
- Duties: define the scope of server responsibilities and the daily coverage map
- Flow: walk through pre-meal setup, during-meal service, and post-meal cleanup
- Comms: teach polite phrasing and tactics for handling difficult guests
- Emergencies: rehearse spill response and the complaint escalation path
- Menu Knowledge: train hero dishes and recommended pairings every server can pitch

ai_instructions.task: Produce a restaurant grand-opening marketing plan
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Buzz: run an opening countdown with teaser posters and social posts in the neighborhood
- Activations: design an opening discount and free tasting events to pull foot traffic
- Channels: push through social media, local listings, and neighborhood flyers in parallel
- Budget: allocate paid ads, materials, and extra staffing dollars across the campaign
- Forecast: estimate opening-day traffic and set the revenue target you must hit

ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Theme: build the campaign around a holiday tie-in or a new menu item launch
- Offers: design a promotional discount and a bundled meal that drive average ticket
- Channels: push through delivery apps, social media, and the email list together
- Measurement: track redemption rate and new loyalty members acquired during the run
- Cost: model margin impact per offer and total profit contribution at end of campaign
- Statuses: define clean, dirty, maintenance, and on-hold statuses with clear criteria
- Refresh: sync status in real time across PMS and standardize the shift-change handoff
- Booking: handle room lock and release rules and define the oversell playbook
- Handoff: build the shift change checklist and the system operation steps every clerk follows
- Overtime Alert: notify on late checkout and apply the charge logic automatically

ai_instructions.task: Produce a hotel digital transformation plan
framework: - Tools: select the PMS, CRM, and POS systems and confirm they integrate cleanly
- Digitize: move the booking flow online and roll out a housekeeping dispatch app
- Analytics: build guest persona models and stand up revenue management on real data
- Training: train staff on the systems and coach the digital mindset alongside the tools
- ROI: total investment cost, project the efficiency lift, and calculate payback

ai_instructions.task: Produce a guest review management plan
framework: - Collection: pull OTA reviews on a schedule and run a guest survey at checkout
- Analysis: cluster positive and negative themes and rank keyword frequencies
- Replies: send a thank-you reply for positives and an issue response for every negative
- Tracking: implement the fix and verify resolution with the guest who raised it
- Trend: chart NPS change month over month and calculate the ROI of each action taken
- Cleaning: define dust removal, sanitization steps, and the linen change protocol for every room turn
- Restock: replenish amenities and refill water bottles before the next guest checks in
- Response: set the room service ticket time and the maintenance request response window in writing
- Complaint: lay out the escalation path and the compensation guidelines for each severity tier
- Checklist: list every item to verify and the supervisor sign-off required before the room goes live
- Greeting: write the welcome script and set the smile and eye-contact standard every clerk follows
- Check-in: define the ID verification step and the key issuance flow from arrival to room
- Check-out: notify housekeeping on departure and present the invoice clearly with itemized charges
- Concierge: train local recommendations and directional guidance so every clerk can answer on the spot
- Phone: set the answer protocol and the transfer-and-hold standard with target ring counts

ai_instructions.type: Crisis Response → trust rebuild, damage control
framework: - Intake: log the complaint channel and timestamp the moment it lands in the system
- Soothe: deliver the empathy script and a sincere apology in language the guest can feel
- Fix: present remedy options and apply compensation tiers based on severity and impact
- Follow-up: confirm the outcome and run a satisfaction check before closing the ticket
- Escalation: hand off to a manager and notify headquarters when the case crosses defined thresholds

ai_instructions.type: Crisis Response → trust rebuild, damage control
framework: - Fire: place extinguishers per code and post the emergency egress map at every floor and elevator
- Security: tighten access control and run the patrol cadence around the clock with logged checkpoints
- Food Safety: run sample testing and check expiry dates daily across every prep station and storeroom
- Emergencies: write fire, earthquake, and medical response protocols and train every shift on each one
- Drills: hold drills annually at minimum and retain drill logs for compliance audits

ai_instructions.task: Produce a hotel loyalty program design
framework: - Tiers: design Silver, Gold, and Platinum tiers with clear qualification criteria for each
- Benefits: set the discount percentage and define room upgrade eligibility per tier
- Points: set the earning rate per dollar spent and the redemption value of each point
- Upgrade Path: define the spend threshold and the maintenance requirement to keep status
- Pricing: set the paid membership fee where applicable and the renewal terms in plain language

ai_instructions.type: Crisis Response → trust rebuild, damage control
framework: - Detection: monitor negative buzz online and set complaint surge alerts that wake the right people
- Response: verify facts fast and develop a unified message before any spokesperson goes public
- Recovery: deliver compensation, a public apology, and a visible root-cause fix to rebuild trust
- Media: designate the spokesperson and prepare the statement template before the next press call lands
- Revenue: set average daily rate, occupancy, and RevPAR targets for the planning year
- Cost: track energy spend, labor cost, and procurement spend against budget every month
- Service: set the customer satisfaction target and run a mystery shopper program quarterly
- Marketing: define distribution channels and the loyalty program's contribution to bookings
- NPS: set the target NPS score and the repeat-stay rate you commit to deliver
- Booking: define the channel mix and capture guest preferences at the moment of reservation
- Check-in/out: standardize the registration flow and the luggage handling steps from curb to room
- Room/Dining: define the room service standard and the turn-down service ritual for evening
- Complaint: train the empathy response and set the escalation path when issues outgrow the front desk
- Post-Stay: deliver the invoice promptly and run a satisfaction follow-up within 48 hours of departure
- Standards: define surface cleaning standards and the corner detail standards for every zone
- Procedure: set cleaning agent ratios and the dwell time required per surface type
- Frequency: set the daily, weekly, and spot-check cadence for each cleaning task
- Owners: assign zone responsibility and require a signature after every clean is verified
- Logs: maintain the cleaning record and the sanitation verification log for audit retrieval
- Receiving: confirm supplier certifications and apply the acceptance criteria on every inbound delivery
- Storage: separate raw and ready-to-eat zones and enforce temperature control with logged checks
- Sampling: define the sampling quantity per lot and the retention sample period required by code
- Staff Health: run a daily check and enforce the sick-leave policy without exception
- Sanitization: set dishwasher temperature minimums and chemical sanitizer concentration targets
- Items: list sanitation checks and food-handling practices to inspect on every audit walk
- Pass/Fail: define conform versus non-conform criteria for each item with measurable thresholds
- Records: log every finding and issue corrective-action requests with named owners and dates
- Follow-up: set the re-check date and verify closure before the audit is marked complete
- Cadence: schedule daily, weekly, and monthly inspections to keep risk low across the board

ai_instructions.task: Produce a food safety training plan
framework: - Content: cover applicable food-safety regulations and the food-handling practices each role must master
- Audience: tailor modules for chefs, servers, and management with role-specific scenarios
- Assessment: combine a written test, a practical demonstration, and a simulation drill to confirm competence
- Refresh: require annual recertification and run new-hire onboarding within the first week

ai_instructions.task: Produce a food safety self-audit report
ai_instructions.type: Routine Report → upward visibility, show value
framework: - Scope: cover high-risk hot zones and the critical control steps that must hold every shift
- Findings: list identified hazards and assign a risk level to each one with supporting evidence
- CAR: issue a corrective action request and set the remediation deadline with a named owner
- Re-check: verify effectiveness on follow-up and keep an open items list visible to the team
- Owners: name the accountable owner and the supervisor reviewing closure before sign-off
- Risk Level: rate each finding high, medium, or low based on urgency and impact severity
- Closure: verify the fix, complete the re-check, and formally close out with documentation

ai_instructions.task: Produce a food safety incident plan
ai_instructions.type: Crisis Response → trust rebuild, damage control
framework: - Severity: classify incidents as minor, major, or critical with clear criteria and trigger thresholds
- Team: stand up incident command and assign medical response roles before the next call drops
- Response: isolate affected product, execute the recall, and notify the regulator within required windows
- Report: file with the regulator on time and prepare a public-facing statement that holds up under scrutiny
- Aftermath: compensate customers, fix the root cause, and run an accountability review to learn

ai_instructions.role: Harvard Graduate School of Education instructional designer
ai_instructions.task: Design a 45-minute lesson plan with clear objectives and assessment
framework: - Subject & Level: name the topic and the grade or age band the lesson serves
- Learning Objective: state what students will be able to do by the end of the lesson
- Hook (5 min): design an engaging opener that ties directly to the lesson's objective
- Direct Instruction (10 min): teach the key concepts with worked examples and clear definitions
- Guided Practice (15 min): run an activity where students work with teacher support and live feedback
- Independent Practice (10 min): give students a task they apply on their own to lock in the skill
- Formative Assessment (5 min): run a quick check for understanding before the bell rings
- Differentiation: build in one accommodation for support and one extension to stretch advanced learners

ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Theme: pick a cross-subject anchor question and explain the value it carries for learners
- Subject Links: bridge knowledge across subjects and define the transfer goals each subject contributes
- Objectives: set knowledge targets and the cross-cutting competencies the unit develops
- Activities: design inquiry tasks and collaborative projects that pull every subject into the work
- Assessment: combine multi-mode evaluation and weight process and product against learning goals
- Resources: pull texts, digital tools, and community partners that deepen the experience
- Tips: leave instructional strategy notes and common-pitfall cautions for the teacher running it

ai_instructions.task: Build a teaching slide deck that's clear, visually clean, and student-friendly
framework: - Deck Structure: title slide, agenda, section dividers, recap slide
- Visual Design: limited color palette, readable font sizes, consistent layout, restrained icon use
- Interaction Beats: pause-and-ask prompts, click-to-reveal answers, quick polls
- Concept Slides: pair text with diagrams or short animations to show, not just tell
- Animations: subtle entrances/exits, motion path used only to direct attention

ai_instructions.task: Produce a complete, well-structured course syllabus
framework: - Course Type: required vs. elective, credit hours, prerequisites
- Content Outline: units, topics, key learning objectives per unit
- Time Allocation: lecture vs. lab/practice hours, weekly hours
- Assessment: weight of participation, assignments, midterm, final
- Reading List: required textbook, supplementary materials, recommended reading

ai_instructions.role: Common Core State Standards lead author
ai_instructions.task: Draft a set of curriculum standards for a subject and grade band
framework: - Course Goals: expected outcomes, competencies students should leave with
- Content Standards: what to teach and how deep students should understand it
- Implementation Notes: recommended teaching strategies, common pitfalls
- Performance Levels: rubric descriptors for each proficiency band, assessment methods
- Academic Quality: knowledge mastery indicators, applied-skill benchmarks

ai_instructions.role: Finnish National Agency for Education curriculum planner
ai_instructions.task: Build a goal-driven, week-by-week teaching plan for a semester
framework: - Weekly Schedule: pacing, what gets covered each week
- Content Map: units and chapters, with priority and tricky topics flagged
- Key Difficulties: which concepts students struggle with, how you'll break them down
- Pacing Buffers: weekly plan plus catch-up days for slippage or review
- Assessment Checkpoints: unit quizzes, midterm, final

ai_instructions.task: Design a lesson hook that grabs attention and leads naturally into the topic
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Hook Type: short story, surprising question, scenario, cliffhanger
- Real-World Frame: a relatable example or visual that anchors the concept
- Driving Question: the one question students should be itching to answer
- Pacing: how long the hook runs, how to keep energy without stalling
- Bridge to Content: how the hook connects to what you're actually teaching

ai_instructions.task: Build a tight microlesson plan with clear segments and a focused learning goal
framework: - Topic & Goal: the single concept being taught and what students will be able to do
- Concept Breakdown: split the idea into 2–4 logical steps in the order students should encounter them
- Script & Visuals: voiceover or narration paired with on-screen captions and visual cues
- Quick Practice: a multiple-choice check, drag-and-drop match, or short prompt mid-lesson
- Run Time: total length, plus how long each segment takes

ai_instructions.task: Write a clear, engaging lecture script that follows a tight logical arc
framework: - Opening: greeting, hook, preview of what students will learn
- Core Explanation: definitions, principles, illustrated with worked examples
- Case Studies: real-world applications woven into the explanation, not bolted on
- Wrap-Up: summary of the 2–3 takeaways, restated in plain language
- Q&A Beats: planned questions to draw students in and check understanding

ai_instructions.task: Build a targeted tutoring plan that addresses a specific student's gaps
framework: - Diagnosis: where the student is now — score patterns, weak topics, error types
- Goals: what we want them to be able to do, by when, and by how much
- Approach: mix of direct teaching, guided practice, and reworking missed problems
- Progress Checks: short tests to measure lift, with checkpoints to adjust the plan
- Family Loop: how parents can support practice and what to flag back to the tutor

ai_instructions.task: Generate a balanced practice question set for a topic
framework: - Question Types: mix of multiple choice, fill-in, and open-response problems
- Difficulty Tiers: foundational warm-ups, core practice, and a few stretch problems
- Topic Coverage: hit each subtopic in the unit, with extra reps on the hardest concepts
- Answer Key & Worked Solutions: full step-by-step reasoning plus common-mistake callouts
- Variations: parallel problems that test the same skill in slightly different framings

ai_instructions.task: Write a complete, well-balanced exam aligned to a topic and difficulty target
framework: - Item Mix: ratio of multiple-choice, short-answer, and free-response items
- Point Allocation: points per item and how the total adds up
- Difficulty Curve: easy / medium / hard split, with attention to discrimination
- Answer Key & Rubric: model answers, scoring criteria, partial-credit rules
- Test Blueprint: a grid mapping each item to the topic and skill it measures

ai_instructions.role: Finnish National Agency for Education homework-design lead
ai_instructions.task: Design a homework set that reinforces what was taught and builds real skill
framework: - Goal: what specific skill or concept this homework should lock in
- Question Mix: foundational practice plus a couple of applied or open-ended problems
- Difficulty Ramp: starts accessible, gets harder, with optional stretch problems
- Grading Criteria: clear rubric with what counts for credit and what feedback to give
- Follow-Up: how you'll review answers in class and support students who struggled

ai_instructions.task: Build a homework set with multiple tiers so every student is appropriately challenged
framework: - Core Tier: required problems every student completes to lock in foundational concepts before moving up
- Growth Tier: optional harder problems for students ready to push past the basics, with light hints if they get stuck
- Challenge Tier: high-difficulty problems for students chasing depth, framed around an interesting application
- Self-Selection: a small menu so students can pick the problem that fits their interest or goal that night
- Grading Rubric: per-tier scoring criteria, mastery indicators, and how partial credit works on stretch work
- Incentives: progress badges, classroom recognition, and end-of-unit shout-outs for stretch attempts
- Feedback Loop: in-class review of common mistakes, targeted one-on-one help, and a short parent update

ai_instructions.task: Write end-of-term narrative comments that are specific, warm, and growth-focused
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Academic Performance: progress in core subjects, study habits, and attitude toward learning
- Character & Behavior: how the student treats classmates, follows class norms, contributes to the room
- Specific Wins: concrete moments — a project, a question asked, a comeback after a tough start
- Growth Edges: what the student should work on, framed as a clear next step rather than a complaint
- Closing Encouragement: a personal note that points forward without being generic

ai_instructions.task: Draft a clear, warm letter from teacher to parents
framework: - Purpose: what the letter is about — academic update, event invite, behavior note, etc.
- Student Snapshot: how the student is doing in class, with both strengths and areas to support
- Ask of the Family: specific things parents can do at home to help
- Contact Info: how to reach the teacher and any class-wide channels (parent group, portal)
- Event Details (if any): what, when, where, what to bring or know

ai_instructions.role: Harvard Graduate School of Education family-engagement expert
ai_instructions.task: Write a parent-night speech that's professional, easy to follow, and warm
framework: - Welcome: thank parents for coming, set the agenda for the evening
- Class Overview: classroom culture, study habits, how the group is doing as a whole
- Academic Picture: grade-distribution snapshot, what's been taught, what's coming next
- Family Partnership: concrete ways parents can support learning at home
- Q&A: collect questions in advance plus open the floor for live ones

ai_instructions.task: Build a focused review plan ahead of a test or end-of-unit assessment
framework: - Scope: which units and topics to cover, with priorities on the trickiest material
- Schedule: daily tasks plus checkpoint quizzes spaced through the review period
- Method: concept maps, cumulative practice, going back through missed problems
- Self-Check: short practice quizzes and a full mock exam toward the end
- Gap Closing: identify weak spots and assign extra targeted practice

ai_instructions.role: Finnish National Agency for Education teaching-summary standards specialist
ai_instructions.task: Write an end-of-term teaching summary that's honest, reflective, and forward-looking
ai_instructions.type: Routine Report → upward visibility, show value
framework: - Goal Achievement: what you set out to teach and how much of it landed
- Bright Spots: methods that worked unusually well, with one or two case examples
- Honest Reflection: what didn't work, why, and what you'd change next time
- Action Plan: concrete adjustments for next term, owners and timelines
- Supporting Data: assessment results, participation rates, sample student work
- Career Focus: name target occupations and target industry sectors the program prepares students for
- Competencies: define technical skills and professional behaviors graduates must demonstrate
- Modules: structure theory units, hands-on practice, and a capstone project across the program
- Work-Based Learning: arrange internship placements and the apprenticeship structure with employers
- Credentials: align with industry-recognized certifications and design stackable credentials
- Goal: set the skilled-worker output target and the post-program placement rate you commit to
- Curriculum: build core knowledge modules and the professional skill units that drive job readiness
- Practice: schedule lab time and the on-the-job apprenticeship that completes the training
- Placement: build the employer partner network and design the cohort program for each track
- Employer Partnerships: secure shared lab investment and run the dual-track model with named partners
- Metrics: track placement rate, skill-match rate, starting wage, and employer satisfaction every cohort
- CI: build the feedback loop and update curriculum and processes based on what the data shows

ai_instructions.role: German Chamber of Industry and Commerce (IHK) vocational-qualification standards specialist
framework: - Methods: combine a written exam with a hands-on demonstration to assess every competency claimed
- Credentialing: define the qualification awarded and the tier levels candidates can achieve
- Portfolio: list the required project deliverables that go into each candidate's portfolio
- Articulation: set credit transfer agreements with colleges and define exemption pathways
- Records: retain the attendance log and practicum reports for the period required by the awarding body

ai_instructions.task: Produce an interactive online course design
framework: - Positioning: name the target audience and the learning goals the course commits to deliver
- Knowledge Map: sequence modules and lay out the progression of difficulty across the course
- Video Structure: set segment lengths and the total duration per module to match attention budgets
- Supplements: provide PDF readings and practice exercises that reinforce each module's core ideas
- Path: mark required modules and recommended additions so learners can self-route by goal

ai_instructions.task: Produce an online course video spec
framework: - Duration: target 5-15 minutes per video and define segment breaks within longer recordings
- Visuals: combine animation and a picture-in-picture instructor frame to keep attention high
- Pacing: set the speech rate and call out emphasis on key points the instructor must hit
- Captions & Interactions: sync captions and place comment-prompt overlays at planned beats
- Brand: define the color palette, font, and lower-third template used across every video

ai_instructions.task: Produce a K-12 online tutoring lesson
framework: - Concept: walk through the formula or rule and run a worked example end to end
- Examples: lay out solved steps in order and design the hint that nudges without giving away the answer
- Practice: build knowledge checks and use interactive question types that keep students engaged
- Q&A: stock the common FAQ and standardize how tutors reply in the comment thread
- Homework: assign a reinforcement set plus a stretch challenge for students ready to push further

ai_instructions.task: Produce an online assessment item bank
framework: - Item Types: include multiple choice, true/false, and fill-in-the-blank items in the bank
- Difficulty: tag each item easy, medium, or hard and track its discrimination index over time
- Tags: align every item to a chapter and a learning-objective tag for fast retrieval
- Rationale: write an answer key with explanation and run distractor analysis on every item
- Random Draw: define the blueprint for assembly and the anti-cheat logic for live administration

ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Quick Q: design multiple-choice questions with buzzer-style timing to drive fast engagement
- Poll: run an opinion vote and show live aggregated stats to spark discussion in the room
- Discussion: post discussion prompts and pin the best comments to model strong contributions
- Rewards: award experience points, badges, and a leaderboard slot for active participation
- Live Feedback: show a correct or incorrect indicator and reveal the explanation immediately

ai_instructions.task: Produce an online learning path design
framework: - Sequence: lay out the prerequisite and post-requisite chain across every module in the path
- Prerequisites: name the prior knowledge and foundational skills learners need before they start
- Duration: set the weekly hour expectation and the total program hours to manage commitment
- Tracking: report completion status and pass criteria for every module and milestone
- Recommendations: smart-push the next module and fill knowledge gaps based on assessment data
- Personalization: run a diagnostic assessment and use intelligent recommendations to route each learner
- Outcomes: measure mastery level, skill lift, and whether the learner hit their stated goal

ai_instructions.task: Produce an online student FAQ doc
framework: - FAQs: cover platform questions and content questions in the categories learners actually search
- Answers: write detailed step-by-step answers and add screenshots where they save explanation time
- Resources: link video tutorials and reference docs that go deeper than the FAQ allows
- Contact: list support email and the help-center forum where learners can reach a human
- Versioning: stamp the last-updated date and keep a changelog of every revision

ai_instructions.task: Produce an online learning engagement plan
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Points: award time-on-platform points and task-completion points to drive the right behaviors
- Badges: design achievement badges and assign rarity tiers that signal real accomplishment
- Leaderboard: publish a weekly time-spent or XP leaderboard to make progress visible across the cohort
- Certificates: issue a verifiable digital certificate with a share link learners can post
- Social: track streaks and offer friend invites so learning becomes a social commitment

ai_instructions.task: Produce an online learner feedback analysis
framework: - CSAT: report course rating and recommendation likelihood across every active cohort
- Suggestions: collect course content suggestions and feature requests and group them by theme
- Plan: rank changes by priority and set a target date for each one in the backlog
- Response: communicate closure to the learner who raised it and run a follow-up satisfaction check
- NPS: measure perceived value and the word-of-mouth indicator across the program

ai_instructions.task: Produce a course landing page copy
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Highlights: lead with the hero content and instructor credentials that justify the price
- Audience: name the prerequisite skills and the target learner profile the course is built for
- Outcomes: list the skills gained and the credential awarded on completion
- Buy CTA: present a time-limited offer and a free preview to lower the buying barrier
- Pricing: state list price, discount, and installment plan options in clear language

ai_instructions.task: Produce an online course trailer brief
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Positioning: state the brand promise and the differentiation that sets this course apart
- Script: build the storyline arc and the emotional hook that pulls viewers through the trailer
- Style: define the color palette and the transition style that ties the trailer to the course brand
- CTA: include a QR code and a course-page jump link so viewers can act inside ten seconds
- Channels: target the social platforms and video platforms where the audience already lives

ai_instructions.role: Council for Exceptional Children assistive-tech expert
framework: - Tools: select AAC devices and speech-output technology that match each student's communication profile

- Environment: design the seating arrangement and manage distractions to lower sensory load

- Home-School: build a regular feedback loop and synchronize strategy between teachers and family
- Assessment: capture the baseline skill profile and document identified needs with current data
- Goals: set short-term and long-term goals with measurable criteria for each one
- Strategies: use differentiated instruction and individualized supports tied to each goal
- Family: design home-practice activities and the parent supports that reinforce the school plan
- Monitoring: set the data collection cadence and schedule retrospective reviews every quarter
- Goals: set inclusion targets and the equity outcomes the school commits to over the year
- Supports: assign resource teachers and confirm itinerant specialist availability across grade levels
- Teacher PD: schedule professional development on inclusive instruction with real classroom application
- Home-School: run parent training sessions and set the regular communications cadence with families

- Encouragement: affirm effort visible in the child's work and name specific strengths the parent can see
- Prompts: ask specific open questions and invite joint problem-solving rather than handing down answers
- Parent: connect parents to emotional support resources and offer referral options when needed
- Regulation: practice active listening and use positive framing to keep the conversation productive
- Student: document present level of performance and the diagnosis driving the need for the IEP
- Goals: write annual goals and break them into short-term benchmarks the team can track
- Services: list the special-education services and related services the student receives
- Assessment: set the progress-monitoring schedule and the end-of-year evaluation method
- Team: name the parent and teacher members alongside the specialist team supporting the plan
- Tools: combine rating scales, observation, and standardized assessment to build the evaluation
- Current Skills: profile cognitive, language, motor, and social skills with current data
- Needs: rank priority needs and the intervention intensity each one requires
- Recommendations: propose goals, instructional strategies, and the placement that best fits
- Follow-up: set the review cadence and the re-evaluation timeline per regulation
- CC/HPI: capture the chief complaint, the symptom trigger, and the clinical course to date
- PMH: list chronic conditions, prior surgeries, and known allergies with reaction notes
- PE: record vital signs and the physical-exam findings that bear on the working diagnosis
- Labs/Imaging: report lab results and any imaging or pathology findings that inform care
- Impression: state the working diagnosis and the differential diagnoses still on the table
- Time: log the visit date and the duration of the conversation
- Topics: document the diagnosis discussed and the treatment plan covered with the patient
- Informed Consent: walk through risks and benefits and review the alternatives openly
- Signatures: capture patient or family signature alongside the physician signature for the record
- Outcome: confirm patient understanding and set the follow-up appointment before they leave
- Orders: list the prescriptions written and any labs ordered for the visit
- Clinical Context: state the diagnosis and the rationale that justifies each order
- Rx Notes: cover dose, duration, and the important cautions for every prescription
- Labs: explain the purpose of each test and the prep instructions the patient must follow
- Follow-up: tell the patient when to return and what to bring or report at that visit
- Aim: state the research question and explain its clinical significance in plain terms
- Methods/Results: describe the study design and the primary findings without burying the lead
- Conclusions: summarize the contribution to the literature and the clinical implications it carries
- Clinical Takeaway: name the practical application and how to disseminate it to colleagues
- Limitations: call out sample size constraints and methodological caveats readers should weigh
- Background: describe the current state of the field and cite the key prior reviews
- Synthesis: organize studies by theme and summarize the key papers in each cluster
- Advances: highlight recent breakthroughs and the theoretical contributions they bring
- Future: identify knowledge gaps and the research frontiers worth pursuing next
- Controversies: surface areas of disagreement and the unresolved evidence still in play
- Flagged: name the out-of-range item and report the measured value clearly
- Reference: state the normal range and any borderline thresholds that affect interpretation
- Meaning: explain the clinical significance in plain language a patient can understand
- Next Step: recommend the specialty referral and any additional workup that's warranted
- Follow-up: tell the patient when to recheck and the warning signs to watch for in the meantime

ai_instructions.role: American Hospital Association clinical-pathway standards specialist
framework: - Condition: name the target diagnosis and the disease classification the pathway covers
- Inclusion: define patient eligibility criteria and the exclusion list with clear thresholds
- Stages: lay out admission, treatment, and rehabilitation phases with expected milestones
- Orders: list required orders, optional orders, and the expected cadence at each phase
- Treatment: cover medications, surgical options, and physical therapy that fit the pathway
- Variances: define how to identify deviations and the escalation path when one occurs
- Discharge: set the symptom criteria and lab criteria the patient must meet for release

ai_instructions.task: Produce a detailed clinical practice guideline
framework: - Condition: define the scope and list the subtypes the guideline addresses
- Type: clarify whether this is a care pathway or a standard practice statement
- Evidence: tag each recommendation with a GRADE level A, B, C, or D based on evidence strength
- Recommendations: lay out diagnostic and treatment steps with clinician-ready specificity
- Population: address adult, pediatric, and special populations as separate sections
- Version: keep a revision history and clear change notes for every published update
- Dx: state the nursing diagnosis and the contributing factors driving it
- Goals: define target outcomes and the evaluation criteria you'll measure against
- Interventions: list the nursing actions and the frequency each one runs at
- Evaluation: confirm whether the goal was met and adjust the plan if it wasn't
- Education: deliver discharge teaching and self-care instruction the patient can follow at home
- Assessment: capture the functional baseline and the impairment list with measurable scores
- Goals: set short-term and long-term goals that are specific and measurable
- Plan: define therapy content, frequency, and duration across the rehab episode
- Training: combine motor training, occupational therapy, and physical modalities as indicated
- Home: write the home-exercise program and safety guidance the patient takes home
- Progress: schedule re-assessment cadence and adjust the plan based on what the data shows
- Outcomes: report functional improvement and quality-of-life metrics at discharge
- Timing: set the post-op day 7 and month 1 visit cadence with concrete dates
- Workups: schedule the imaging and lab follow-ups required at each visit
- Meds: lay out the titration plan and the dosing schedule the patient follows
- Mode: offer in-clinic, phone, and telehealth options based on what the visit requires

ai_instructions.role: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health nutrition professor
ai_instructions.task: Produce a 7-day meal plan hitting stated calorie and macro targets
framework: - Person: capture sex, age, weight, activity level, and goal — lose, maintain, or gain weight
- Daily Targets: set calorie target plus protein, carb, and fat ranges for each day
- Dietary Constraints: list allergies, food preferences, and the cuisine style the plan will lean into
- Day 1-7: lay out breakfast, lunch, dinner, and one or two snacks for each day of the week
- Shopping List: consolidate ingredients by aisle so one trip covers the whole week
- Prep Tips: identify which items to batch cook on the weekend to save time on weeknights
- Body Type: assess the physiological constitution and document the current presentation
- Principles: balance across pillars and tailor the plan to the individual's life and constraints
- Diet/Exercise: recommend food-as-medicine guidance and a movement plan that fits the schedule
- Sleep: set a regular schedule and the target sleep duration the body needs to recover
- Mind: build in stress management and offer music or art therapy options for emotional balance

ai_instructions.role: American College of Sports Medicine certified rehab specialist
framework: - Phase: distinguish acute phase work from recovery phase work with clear progression criteria
- Mode: balance active versus passive work and strength versus flexibility across each session
- Intensity: set a rate of perceived exertion target and the weekly frequency that drives results
- Contraindications: name dangerous movements and painful regions to avoid until cleared
- Goals: set the strength target and the range-of-motion target with measurable thresholds
- Movement Breakdown: describe the starting position and the correct form cues for each exercise
- Schedule: lay out the daily session and the weekly progression plan across the program
- Assessment: name the measurement tool and the target threshold the patient must reach
- Progression: define the incremental difficulty increase and the criteria to advance

ai_instructions.role: Andrew Weil Center for Integrative Medicine director
framework: - Principles: balance across body systems and tailor the approach to the individual's profile
- Therapies: combine herbal supplements with acupuncture options where evidence supports it
- Lifestyle: prescribe diet adjustments and stress management practices the patient can sustain
- Duration: set the course length and the follow-up cadence across the treatment plan
- Awareness: explain the causes and the possible complications in language the patient understands
- Meds: stress adherence and walk through the common side effects to watch for
- Lifestyle: cover diet, exercise, and smoking cessation with specific actions, not vague advice
- Self-Monitoring: guide blood pressure, blood glucose, and weight tracking with target ranges
- When to Seek Care: list red-flag symptoms and the scheduled visits the patient must keep

ai_instructions.task: Produce a continuous chronic care program
framework: - Goals: set the target metric to control and the quality-of-life lift you commit to deliver
- Interventions: combine medication regimen with behavioral coaching tied to the goal
- Follow-up: schedule phone calls, in-clinic visits, and home visits across the care episode
- Measurement: track goal attainment and patient satisfaction at every checkpoint
- Alerts: define the hypoglycemia protocol and the hypertensive emergency response in writing
- Adherence: deploy monitoring tools, reminders, and incentive programs that move behavior
- Tech: equip the patient with remote monitoring devices, a mobile app, and analytics support
- Factors: weigh genetic predisposition, lifestyle, and environmental exposures against each condition
- Result: report the risk tier and the absolute risk percentage for the period assessed
- Interventions: recommend screening cadence and prevention measures tailored to each risk
- Re-assess: set the next assessment date and the trigger for early re-evaluation
- Health Age: report physiological age versus chronological age to make risk concrete
- Items: list the base panel and the specialty add-ons available in the package
- Audience: tailor by age and sex and account for occupational considerations
- Flow: walk through scheduling, fasting prep, and the test order on the day of the visit
- Interpretation: explain what each result means and the follow-up if anything comes back abnormal
- Advice: deliver lifestyle recommendations and specialist referrals when the results call for it
- Price: state the bundle price and the add-on pricing in plain language
- Booking: enable phone or app booking and send preparation instructions ahead of the visit
- Goal: name the target behavior to change and the target metric value you aim to hit
- Actions: lay out specific actions and the scheduled cadence for each one
- Support: list the tools provided and the professional guidance the participant can lean on
- Evaluation: measure pre and post and run interval checks across the program
- Milestones: schedule weekly check-ins and a monthly review to keep momentum visible

ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Topic: pick the prevention focus and state the health-literacy goal the doc serves
- Core: ground the content in current science and cite the evidence behind every claim
- Behavior: translate science into actionable steps with step-by-step guidance the reader can follow
- Myths: surface common myths in the area and offer factual corrections with sources
- Q&A: build a self-quiz and real-life scenarios that let readers test their understanding
- Disease Info: cover pathology basics and the prognosis range in plain, honest language
- Treatment: walk through prescriptions, surgical options, and rehabilitation pathways
- Self-Mgmt: guide diet adjustments and the role of mood and stress in recovery
- Mental Support: offer anxiety guidance and point to support resources beyond the clinic
- Rehab: lay out the home-care plan and the follow-up schedule the patient must keep

ai_instructions.task: Produce a health management service package
framework: - Services: bundle assessment, intervention, and follow-up into a single service offering
- Audience: target chronic-condition members and the high-risk segment that benefits most
- Flow: walk through enrollment and the service delivery cadence over the year
- Pricing: set the annual fee and the payment options members can choose from
- Quality: define service standards and the complaint handling process when issues arise
- Team: name the physician lead and the health coach support each member receives
- Add-ons: offer telehealth visits and priority scheduling as premium add-ons
- Org Chart: lay out departments and the reporting hierarchy across the hospital
- Job Desc: define role responsibilities and the decision authority each role carries
- Workflows: map the patient visit flow and the referral process between departments
- KPIs: name the tracked metrics and the review cycle each metric runs on
- QA: stand up a quality assurance program and a continuous improvement cycle behind it
- Events: define incident categories and the reportable events list with clear thresholds
- Report Flow: lay out internal escalation and the external notification process per regulation
- RCA: form the root-cause analysis team and focus on the underlying cause, not the surface event
- Prevention: drive process changes and training updates that close the gap permanently
- Drills: run annual safety drills and tabletop exercises to keep readiness sharp

ai_instructions.type: Routine Report → upward visibility, show value
framework: - Indicators: report mortality rate, complication rate, and other quality indicators tracked
- Stats: aggregate quarterly and annual numbers with trend lines that surface drift
- Issues: identify root causes and assign accountability to a named owner with a date
- Actions: write the remediation plan and track every action through to closure
- Trends: compare month-over-month and year-over-year against target attainment
- Dimensions: weight case volume, quality outcomes, and patient satisfaction across every clinician
- KPIs: quantify metrics and assign a clear weighting to each dimension in the score
- Scoring: document scoring details and the performance tiers each total maps to
- Application: tie the score to bonus payout and promotion eligibility for the period
- Sources: pull data from the hospital information system and structured patient feedback

ai_instructions.task: Draft a payment-reform proposal moving from fee-for-service toward DRG / bundled payments
framework: - Patient Grouping: define MS-DRG assignment logic, episode definitions, and severity and complication adjustments
- Cost & Payment: compare average case cost to the proposed DRG or bundle rate and set outlier and stop-loss provisions
- Performance Impact: project effects on hospital revenue, physician practice patterns, length-of-stay, and readmission risk
- Implementation: drive clinical documentation improvement, coder training, care-pathway redesign, and IT and billing workflow changes
- Evidence Base: ground the proposal in historical claims analysis, comparable program outcomes, and financial simulation under multiple scenarios

ai_instructions.role: American Hospital Association compliance standards specialist
framework: - Items: list quality indicators and infection control checks the audit must cover every cycle
- Regulations: cite applicable laws and industry standards behind each checklist item
- Self-Audit: rate each item conform or non-conform with supporting evidence on file
- CAR: write corrective actions and set realistic deadlines with named owners for each
- Cadence: schedule the annual full audit and the special-purpose audits across the year

ai_instructions.type: Crisis Response → trust rebuild, damage control
framework: - Severity: classify incidents minor, major, or critical with clear thresholds for each tier

- Scripts: prepare empathy language and negotiation framing every responder uses
- Compensation: calculate medical costs and lost wages with a defensible methodology
- Prevention: drive informed-consent improvements and quality system updates that close gaps
- Standards: align with Joint Commission and AHA accreditation standards point by point
- Documentation: assemble policies and procedural records the surveyor will sample
- Plans: assign gap-list owners and write the remediation plan with target dates
- Prep: run a mock survey and archive evidence in a single, navigable repository
- Customer: review credit history and the customer's industry-rank position against peers
- Repayment: assess cash-flow capacity and the leverage profile that supports repayment

- Controls: document the collateral package and the post-loan monitoring requirements
- Collateral: report loan-to-value ratio and the liquidity of every pledged asset
- Application: capture customer name, product type, and the amount requested up front
